name: AWS WAF — Debug and Metrics Endpoint Restriction
objective: Block or tightly scope access to common diagnostics, metrics, and actuator paths that should not be broadly reachable from the internet.
risk_mitigated: Exposure of metrics, health, actuator, or internal diagnostic paths that support reconnaissance, data leakage, and privilege escalation planning.
potential_side_effects: Platform telemetry, uptime monitors, and synthetic probes can be blocked if they rely on public paths instead of authenticated internal routes.
tuning_notes: If diagnostics must remain reachable, prefer an allowlist or authenticated internal network path. Do not globally weaken the path match just to accommodate monitoring.
deployment_notes: Use this rule after inventorying operational probes and platform tooling. Keep production metrics and diagnostics behind private networking or a separate authenticated control plane whenever possible.
monitoring_notes: Track repeated requests to operational paths by ASN and host. A steady trickle of debug endpoint requests from the internet usually indicates opportunistic scanning.
